key_insight: A transfer-learning upgrade of BiSeNetV2 with a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss substantially improves weld seam segmentation robustness under reflective, low-visibility construction welding conditions without increasing model complexity.
engineering_relevance: This offers a lightweight, real-time perception solution that can make autonomous welding robots more reliable on challenging metallic surfaces and in harsh illumination.
method: Fine-tuned a BiSeNetV2 backbone using transfer learning.
Used a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász objective to stabilize training and improve boundary/overlap accuracy.
Focused on reflection robustness rather than adding parameters, FLOPs, or latency.
evidence_signal: The proposed method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by +22.36 points over the OHEM baseline while recovering 96.33% of severe zero-IoU failure cases.
watch_item: Performance gains are shown for specific welding-reflection conditions, so deployment should still be validated across new joint types, materials, and camera/lighting setups.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
